title: Webhooks, Replay Defense, SSRF, Abuse, and Sensitive Business Flows · Goroutine Forensics
summary: An incident responder inherits a plausible but defective webhook authenticity, replay, and abuse defense; retain the failing request and response, isolate the first causal server boundary, and keep a regression probe. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in webhook authenticity, replay, and abuse defense and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Bound and retain exact raw body bytes, select expected signature scheme and key, compare in constant time, and only then parse and authorize the event.
Validate timestamp or nonce window, event ID uniqueness, tenant, type, ordering, duplicate delivery, and durable processing state before effects.
Acknowledge only after durable acceptance, move long work to bounded processing, classify retries, make effects idempotent, and expose operator recovery.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Order the regression proof · Debug Business Flow Abuse
steps.instruction:
Order the Business Flow Abuse Go server HTTP evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Go server Order the regression proof · Debug Business Flow Abuse.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Business Flow Abuse assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Go server Order the regression proof · Debug Business Flow Abuse, keep Business Flow Abuse connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.text: A debug team handles case 3701 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic Go evidence to combine raw-message webhook authentication, replay bounds, and SSRF egress policy; then saturate one admission budget while another tenant remains serviceable. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Bound costly domain actions by principal, resource, workflow state, velocity, inventory, and anomaly evidence beyond raw request rate.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Business Flow Abuse has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Bound costly domain actions by principal, resource, workflow state, velocity, inventory, and anomaly evidence beyond raw request rate.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
